20 September 2025
C
00:36
Crypto Rug Muncher
⚠️ Be careful with “1 coin can change your life” — $1.

I’m seeing the usual suspects push this hard. Some of the same accounts shilling it—like @MarcellxMarcell—have blocked me after I called out their scams in the past.

Here’s the common pattern with these types of projects:

• Allocation goes to KOLs who hype the launch.
• Retail pushes the market cap up.
• KOLs dump into the strength.
• Project fades once the hype cycle ends.

I can’t say that’s definitively what will happen with $1, but it’s the typical outcome for KOL‑driven pumps—especially when promotions are undisclosed. If they’re being paid, they should say so. They rarely do.

Do your own research. For me, this is a pass.

CA: GMvCfcZg8YvkkQmwDaAzCtHDrrEtgE74nQpQ7xNabonk

$1 on Dexscreener:

https://dexscreener.com/solana/6sekz73vph5afymqtzav6h5vaqqlz2c8wqecq6mhlqfk

This might sound worse than I want it to be - but Social Media is a lot of seeking for engagement and fake it until you make it. A story that you got rugged sells quickly as any of us feel like it can happen to any of us anytime as well! And seeing >10k$ drained from a wallet leaves us speechless. I don't want to say that everyone fakes their stories (to some people it really happens) but a lot of people really do fake their stories on Social Media to get more engagement, viewership and grow their accounts

00:31
Crypto Rug Muncher
What does our project flywheel look like? It’s simple: you buy, hold, and trade $CRM; we allocate at least 50% of trading fees to build next‑gen security tools for degens. No insiders. No KOLs. No bundles. Just a clean, community‑driven model.

Utility isn’t always “sexy,” but execution here is straightforward if the community keeps trading. Consider $PEPE on @BagsApp: over $450k in fees traded into the project at roughly a $920k market cap—and @Matt_Furie hasn’t even collected his fees yet. If he does, that value likely exits the ecosystem forever.

$CRM is different. We’re the only creator coin on Bags explicitly building security products for the community, and the first Bags project registered as an LLC—real structure, real accountability.

Bottom line: if you want a project that converts trading volume into tangible tools that protect traders, get involved in $CRM. Buy, hold, trade—and help fund the security stack degens actually need. This is your chance to back a team that is buidling, a treasury that reinvests, and a community that owns its outcomes. Let’s build, win, and set the standard for what a creator coin can be.

CA: Eme5T2s2HB7B8W4YgLG1eReQpnadEVUnQBRjaKTdBAGS

01:14
Crypto Rug Muncher
The silver lining of the $ASTER and $BNB chaos? A few BNB whales are trading under me on GMGN. Earning from their trading fees has been an unexpected surprise this week.

Why do whales seem to prefer GMGN?

• Cross‑chain coverage: one dashboard to hunt opportunities and risks across chains.
• Fast, reliable data: low‑latency mempool and on‑chain signals that actually fire in time.
• Wallet intelligence: auto‑labels KOL/dev wallets and links related addresses/clusters.
• Smart‑money tracking: see entries/exits, size, and PnL of top performers.
• Real‑time alerts: whales, fresh wallets, and sniper activity as it happens.
• Rug/scam heuristics: contract risk flags, liquidity locks, ownership checks, and mint/blacklist detection.
• Trade workflow: streamlined UI, quick filters, and integrations that make entries/exits faster.

If you’re still flying blind on BNB, don’t. Use my GMGN ref link below to stay ahead of scams and protect your bags.

https://gmgn.ai/r/rugmunch?chain=bsc

C
05:05
Crypto Rug Muncher
$CRM is a giant middle finger to the pay-to-play crypto machine.

Yeah, the website sucks in its current form and we’re not on CMC/CG—yet. So why stick around and why get involved now?

We’ve delivered on our other commitments and we show up daily. Four weeks of building in public, transparent accounting, consistent updates, and execution—resulting in a ~$400k market cap with a strong community and a solid floor.

Structure and transparency

• LLC formed: real accountability, real partnership possibilities, real execution.
• Consistent AMAs: open mic for the community. Anyone can speak, ask hard questions, and challenge us. Your feedback feeds the roadmap.

Website: built the right way, paid piecemeal
We’re not slapping together a template. We’re doing it right—and yes, it isn’t cheap.

• Phase 1 (Design): paid in full. Done.
• Phase 2 (Coding): in progress. Developer has been paid roughly half; remaining balance due on delivery.
• Phase 3 (Animations): queued next. Same developer will handle it.

* Our web developer has worked with top crypto projects and is building to that standard. Finishing the site is a huge priority for me personally, and we’re funding it step-by-step as volume allows so we don’t rug our own runway.

Why aren’t CMC/CG live?

The “free” route isn’t free for small builders. This industry nickel‑and‑dimes you from day one. We’ve refused the greasy backchannel game for listings. Our model is honest: development is funded by trading volume. When volume dips, we protect liquidity, push marketing, and volume—so we don’t die off. That takes precedence over greasing palms.

How you can help

• Trade $CRM: more volume = faster delivery (including the website phases and listings)
• Spread the word: bring in traders who care about security
• Show up: join AMAs, shape the roadmap, keep us accountable

Bottom line

If volume doesn’t cover our needs, the whales and I will. Either way, we move forward. This project is a giant middle finger to the money‑grabbers who exploit retail every day. We’re here to ship community‑funded scam‑detection tools that protect everyone in this space—and prove it’s feasible without insider games. If this resonates, join us. If you’re tired of pay‑to‑play crypto and want builders who fight for the community, back the creator coin that fights with you. Support $CRM.

CA: Eme5T2s2HB7B8W4YgLG1eReQpnadEVUnQBRjaKTdBAGS
🔥 15 ❤ 2 👍 1

Crypto Rug Muncher
What do I see when I look at the $CRM chart? Four weeks of consistent grind from ~1,200 holders who give a damn about crypto security and are done getting rugged. Next month’s goal: find another 1,200 who want an app that publicly nut‑kicks the very KOLs who’ve been fleecing them for years. Victims are everywhere—and most are ready to do something about it. We just need to reach them and show them what we’re building. Keep pushing. $CRM is here to stay. Please join us.

06:43
Crypto Rug Muncher
Many have asked how turning the work I do on X into Rug Munch Media LLC benefits $CRM and $cryptorugmunch holders. Today we have at least one answer for you. Effective today, at least 50% of fees earned from my trading‑platform partnerships and 50% from Rug Pull Rehab courses will fund our community‑run crypto project—fueling regular buybacks/burns, key deliverables, liquidity/volume support, targeted ads, and finishing what we started for the community.

All revenue from these new streams will be sent to rugmunchdao.sol weekly. The community will be able to see deposits and track spending on an ongoing basis. For transparency, we’ll publish weekly earnings reports for every revenue stream we implement going forward (when revenue is earned), plus a clear breakdown of how these funds are allocated to the project.

If you’re trading, there’s a good chance you’re already using a platform we partner with. And there’s an even better chance you signed up through a scamfluencer’s referral link—funding their fat ass to this very day.

Stop funding scammers. Open a new trading account with a project that builds for the little guy and flags scams and scammers regularly. By using our referral links below, you’ll support next‑gen, community‑funded scam‑detection tools—while trading on the platforms you already love and know. We appreciate your support, and you can be confident your fees will go to a legit cause that benefits the whole space.

GMGN: https://gmgn.ai/r/rugmunch

AXIOM: https://axiom.trade/@crmunch

Trojan: https://t.me/solana_trojanbot?start=r-cryptorugmunch

Photon: https://photon-sol.tinyastro.io/@cryptorugmunch

Bloom🌸: https://t.me/BloomSolana_bot?start=ref_YL9NHJBS9A

BullX: https://t.me/BullxBetaBot?start=access_VD9LU4H5I2D

Telemetry (by BONKbot): https://app.telemetry.io/@2dx3m

Interested in my Rug Pull Rehab class? DM me on X or Telegram @CryptoRugMunch.

Pricing

• 1 SOL for a 2-hour private lesson (held and recorded on Google Meet)
• 0.5 SOL for group lessons (offered based on interest; details to follow)

Scheduling

• If weekend group classes run, they’ll likely be once per week on Saturday or Sunday. I’ll share final details soon.
• Private lessons can be booked at the student’s convenience.

Notes

• I’ll make a separate post outlining the details of what is covered in the course curriculum for anyone who is interested.
• The course has been updated to reflect current market conditions as well as notable events of the 2024/2025 market. Many past students have told me the course delivers strong value for the information provided.

Why commit 50% of the personal revenue I earn through this page to the project? Because leadership means skin in the game. A good captain steers for clear waters—and if not, he goes down with the ship. I’m putting real stakes behind this project so the community knows I’m serious about moving it forward. I believe good attracts good, and this commitment is my invitation to builders and believers to join me. I’ve put my reputation on the line—now I’m putting my money on the line, too. Ride or die.

We’ll keep innovating and finding new ways to create holder value through the LLC. This is just the start. Stay tuned—because we’re here to build, deliver, and prove—month after month—that your trust compounds into real, measurable value for those who give us a chance and back us in these early days of building.

Crypto Rug Muncher
We need the community—now more than ever. If $CRM is to make it as a project, we need more voices in chat and on the timeline. There are nearly 1,200 holders, but not enough engagement to drive the momentum and volume we need at the moment.

If you want $CRM to succeed, put in a little work for your bag:

• Post about what we’re building. Share updates and wins.
• Join the community chat, stay active, and join raids.
• Show up to AMAs, ask questions, and participate.

This is how we win: consistent, visible support from the people who believe. Be the community you want this project to be. Let’s show up and push together. I’ll be here every day—can I count on you to show up, too?

09:53
Crypto Rug Muncher
⚠️ Watch out for the account @ansemsol0x — he’s promoting scams and then dirty-deleting the evidence after his crime is complete. He’s also posing as @blknoiz06. His most recent scam was WhaleWifHat - $WHALEWIF earlier tonight. He left the liquidity on this project unlocked and drained it completely within seconds. With the rise of Pump.fun this type of scam isn’t as popular these days, but they still happen.

Remember to always check whether a project’s liquidity is locked before you buy. Dexscreener will place a lock icon over every project that has its liquidity locked, so this isn’t difficult to check — but it should be checked every time.

Crypto Rug Muncher
Why will our project succeed? Because the moment all the KOL larps / scammers catch even the slightest whiff that the trenches are alive again, they’ll come back in full force. At the same time, new people will enter the space—new victims—who get rugged, find us, and want payback.

One core feature we’re planning on building: a definitive “naughty list” for every KOL we can track—logging their calls, measuring performance over time, and visualizing it in a clean, easy-to-read interface. If you just got scammed, why wouldn’t you support a project that’s promising transparency and accountability for the rats who fleeced you?

You’re early. Get $CRM.

11:55
Crypto Rug Muncher
Multi-platform live streaming will be happening this week for our project. We’ll cover daily crypto news, ongoing scams and scammers, community requests for project analysis, and interviews with people from across the space. I’ll host to start, and I may bring in other creators over time. If you’re into videography and fighting scams, reach out if you are interested in getting involved. These channels will back up what we already do on X and Telegram and help to expand and grow the $CRM / $cryptorugmunch community.

What you might not know about me personally: I’ve been a teacher for over 20 years, including coaching classes full of the most powerful men in companies like Samsung and Hyundai in South Korea, but I’m an introvert in my personal life. I’ve already pushed myself outside my comfort zone by doing weekly AMAs on X since my project started. I’m going to be pushing myself again by going live regularly across multiple platforms.

This project is about trying new things and stepping outside my comfort zone—for me and for the space. We’re here to show you can run a crypto project well, for the community, without fleecing anyone.

I’m committed to showing up, building in public, and keeping it transparent. If you want a fairer crypto space, stand with us, tune in, and help set the standard. Let’s prove that integrity and execution win.

First stream announcement drops after tomorrow’s AMA. I’m excited for this next chapter in the @CryptoRugMunch saga and hope you’ll be there with me.

Youtube: https://youtube.com/@CryptoRugMuncher

Twitch: https://twitch.tv/cryptorugmunch

Kick: https://kick.com/cryptorugmunch

Rumble: https://rumble.com/c/c-7786852

12:49
Crypto Rug Muncher
In the banner of my X account it says “SEND US RUGS TO MUNCH @CRYPTORUGMUNCH” and I mean it. If you come across an ongoing scam or a habitual scammer the community needs to be alerted about, send the info my way—ideally with evidence if you have it. The more scams and scammers we crowdsource, the more we can stop. DMs are open on X and Telegram: @CryptoRugMunch.

Note: Sending info doesn’t guarantee I’ll cover it. I try to weigh a variety of factors before deciding if something is worth the time to pursue or not. When the market is hot it’s hard to get to everything, but when things are slow I can get through a lot more requests. Send your tips my way. Anonymity is 100% guaranteed.

Crypto Rug Muncher
The difference between me and other devs? I’m up at 1 AM bull-posting about my project—dropping announcements, breaking down features we want to ship in our app—on a Tuesday morning. Meanwhile, most of these jokers can barely eke out a post or two a day a month into their project without getting hit by a barrage of criticism from concerned community members.

We’re not the same—my community trusts me and what I’m building, and the broader market will soon trust our project too. We’ll show up every day, outwork the rest—and we will win. Buy, hold, and trade $CRM with confidence. We're here to support the community and we’re here to stay.

Crypto Rug Muncher
It’s been a while since I covered Finixio / Clickout Media, so let’s see what presale scams they’re pushing now. Reminder: these folks run a media network that includes Cryptonews.com, 99Bitcoins, Techopedia, and more—mixing legit-looking news with their own promo scams.

Current projects to avoid if you value your funds:

Bitcoin Hyper ($HYPER)
Maxi Doge ($MAXI)
PepeNode ($PEPENODE)
Wall Street Pepe ($WEPE)
Snorter Bot ($SNORT)
Best Wallet Token ($BEST)
SUBBD ($SUBBD)
Little Pepe ($LILPEPE)
SpacePay ($SPY)
BlockchainFX ($BFX)
Pumpd ($PUMPD)

I’ve tracked these companies for well over a year. Every project they touch follows the same scam playbook. The fact they’re still operating after extracting millions from retail suggests regulators—and many participants in the space—either aren’t paying attention or don’t care. Protect yourself: avoid these media companies and anything they promote. Stay safe.

Crypto Rug Muncher
Hi @finnbags @BagsApp— we’re still trying to reach you regarding our project $CRM on your app.

We get that you provide the platform and what happens after is largely on creators. That stance might fly if you were the #1 Solana launchpad—you’re not. You’re currently 9th and near the bottom across all key metrics. If you want to climb, back legit builders and do things differently than the rest.

$CRM is, to my knowledge:

• The only project using creator funds to form an LLC
• The only project committing 50% of platform fees to build next-gen scam detection tools for degens

I’m one of the largest creators on your app and I’m trying to do right by my community. Your lack of acknowledgment doesn’t inspire confidence in your direction as a platform.

Do better if you want to be better. We’re ready to work with you—are you ready to back real projects and build a community that actually wants to build with you?

23:39
Durdom
Phishing attempts often use the following tactics

1. Email “From” address impersonation
• Be vigilant about possible omissions or incorrect characters in email addresses.
2. Log-in information requests
• If you get an email or text message (SMS) asking for your crypto Trading Account email, phone, password, or Recovery phrase it most likely is a scam.
3. “Appearing” helpful
• We often see scammers sprinkling through what appears to be “helpful” hints and tips as a decoy tactic.
• They also try to create a false sense of urgency, to act quickly before you’ve had time to think carefully.
4. Using official logos and links
• Many phishing emails will consist of standard company logos and official sounding language to make it appear to be real.
C
23:54
Crypto Rug Muncher
As a snapshot of how corrupt parts of crypto are: since launching my project, a dozen+ big KOLs have DM’d me saying they love the idea and want in. When I tell them all they need to do is buy, hold, or trade $CRM and put in the work for their own bags, the convo dies. For many, it’s only about getting paid—regardless of the project.

Some will probably circle back (maybe some already have). The irony? They will have missed massive gains by just not doing the legwork themselves.

The most wholesome way to win in crypto isn’t dumping on your community—it’s working for your bags. Gatekeepers hate work. We don’t. $CRM

Crypto Rug Muncher
I somehow ended up in this 146k‑member Telegram scam hole. The latest token they’re pushing, $SHREK, looks set to follow the same playbook as previous launches. They don’t always hard‑rug, but 99% of the action happens in the first 24 hours—and then the team starts dumping.

The reality: most of these launches have no future. Without a real team or community post‑launch, they’re basically pump‑and‑dumps. If you value your funds, avoid groups like this. Stay safe out there.

Turning Clout Into Fuel — How Rug Munch Media LLC Pays $CRM and $cryptorugmunch

1/6 — TL;DR I posted this yesterday in one long post. Too chunky. Here’s the full announcement threaded and readable. The original post has also been included below for anyone who wants to read it.

Effective today:

• 50% of fees from my trading-platform partnerships
• 50% of Rug Pull Rehab course revenue

All go back into our community-run project. That means regular buybacks/burns, shipping key deliverables, liquidity + volume support, targeted ads, and finishing what we started for the community.

03:32
Crypto Rug Muncher
The $CRM / $cryptorugmunch project is stepping into the DeFi trading referral game in a big way.

Yesterday I said it, and I’ll repeat it: minimum 50% of all referral earnings go straight back into the project. We’ll compete for flow, call out scammers blood sucking off these platforms, and redirect that money into holder value.

Let’s be crystal clear:

• This is not me telling you to trade.
• This is not an endorsement of any platform.
• Trading = personal responsibility. Know the risks, understand the product, make your own call.

Why we’re doing this:

• People are trading on these platforms regardless of whether I talk about them or not.
• Millions in volume move on these platforms daily.
• A good chunk of the fees these platforms pay out end up with known scammers. If we’re involved, we can capture some of that flow and put it to work transparently for the project as well as take some of the wind out of their sails.

Bottom line: You choose if and where you trade. We’re using referrals as an organic funding stream, backing it with weekly reports, and committing at least 50% back into our project. We’re not pushing trading on anyone—we’re being realistic and pointing to platforms that already exist and are widely used in the ecosystem. Don’t @ me if you get rekt. Personal responsibility is key, and my advice if you don’t know how to trade is simple: don’t.

Referral links for platforms we’ve established partnerships with. If there’s a platform you use and want added, ping me and we’ll spin up a link for you and add it to the list in our next post.

• GMGN: https://gmgn.ai/r/rugmunch
• AXIOM: https://axiom.trade/@crmunch
• Trojan: https://t.me/solana_trojanbot?start=r-cryptorugmunch
• Photon: https://photon-sol.tinyastro.io/@cryptorugmunch
• Bloom: https://t.me/BloomSolana_bot?start=ref_YL9NHJBS9A
• BullX: https://t.me/BullxBetaBot?start=access_VD9LU4H5I2D
• Telemetry (by BONKbot): https://app.telemetry.io/@2dx3m

Reminder: Not financial advice. Trading is personal responsibility. If you don’t understand the risks, don’t trade.

04:16
Crypto Rug Muncher
Why 50% and not 100% in $CRM / $cryptorugmunch-backed ventures?

Short answer: I’m not here to milk anyone. I’m here to build.

I commit a minimum of 50% of earnings back into the project because I don’t know what this revenue will look like over time. If it’s $20 a week, I can’t responsibly funnel 100% of that into the project and pretend the ops, taxes, and time don’t exist. If it ever turns into real money, I’m obviously not pocketing it all and eating a massive tax bill either.

This is why we formed an LLC around our project. It lets us:

• Reinvest into the company when revenue spikes.
• Handle taxes and expenses cleanly.
• Move toward a normal structure where I take a salary, and most of the upside flows back to the community.
• The 50% is a baseline, not a ceiling. As we grow, the goal is more transparency, more reporting, and more returned to holders. I’m setting expectations honestly now so we can scale sustainably later.

$CRM — $cryptorugmunch

10:02
$CRM Chat
In reply to this message
I'm willing to consider this but it's not something I'm willing to immediately agree to. For now I've been placing a lot of focus on $CRM on Sol because this token has the most interest from the community and it's honestly where the possibility of success for this project currently lies. That said, I won't totally give up on Zora yet because I think their platform has the potential to grow with time and the coin is essentially staying around the same market cap and not being traded heavily, no harm, no foul.

I think there will come a time when it's wise to be established on Base. Maybe that moment isn't now and maybe that platform isn't Zora but probably a bit premature to make that call so early into the project and before we've really announced plans for it. The thing with Zora is they will give us 50% of the supply of the token over 5 years. I see it as more of a long term play for the project to have more control over a good portion of our supply on at least one chain we are traded on.

If we do end support we want the funds on hand to make anyone who bought whole again. Same with our Bags contract. If we ever switch contracts or abandon support of the platform we want to be in a place where everyone gets back what they put in if not more on the new contract. Probably the best strategy at the moment would be to keep on keeping on until we are in a place where that can happen and to give us time to see where Zora goes as a platform.
❤

12:29
Crypto Rug Muncher
I think a lot more crypto projects should run like businesses—or at least seriously consider it. A clear model we can point to on this is Pudgy Penguins. They were acquired by a private company as NFT hype was fading, but the new leadership focused on real value: retail distribution (Walmart nationwide), licensable IP, and brand touchpoints beyond CT.

When they launched their memecoin, they rewarded NFT holders and the broader community. Sure, there was a launch dump—as there usually is—but they kept building and delivering. Now $PENGU is one of the best-performing memes of 2025. When incentives are aligned around delivering value to the people who’ve invested in your brand, the crypto side tends to follow along. That’s the alignment this space needs to mature.

Pudgy isn’t a fluke; it’s a blueprint that can be replicated. Treat crypto projects as parts of a broader brand and business, not the entire business. Build products, partnerships, IP, merchandising, and community initiatives that can stand on their own. Tie upside to real-world outcomes and keep rewarding the people who actually show up. Do that across multiple channels and you can weather market cycles, turn momentum into distribution, and distribution into demand. That’s how a project becomes a company, and a company becomes an ecosystem.

This is the route $CRM / $cryptorugmunch is taking. We’re building like a business, not just a chart: diversified revenue, partnerships, products people actually want, and mechanisms that deliver value back to holders—rather than relying on token hype cycles. It may take longer and the early wins can look small, but over time it opens far more possibilities than a standard “bread and butter” crypto project ever could. That’s our path, and it’s how we plan to build something durable for the community that will stand the test of time regardless of market conditions. If you see the vision, we’d love to have you join us.
❤ 16 👍 2 👏 1

Crypto Rug Muncher
Three new GMGN sign-ups today—let’s go! That’s three more traders helping fund our project.

Quick reminder: I’ve pledged at least 50% of all trading platform referral fees back into the $CRM and $cryptorugmunch project to build next-gen security / scam detection tools for the community.

If you hold our token and you’re trading under someone else’s referral, you’re short-changing your own bags. And if you signed up through a scamfluencer's link, that cash is gone from the ecosystem forever. Why not trade under a project that has pledged at least half of your fees towards building on behalf of the community? Put your fees to work—switch to our referral link today.

Check the links below for the platforms we’re partnered with. If your platform isn’t listed, ping me and I’ll add it in the next update. Cheers!

Put your trading fees to work for the community. Use these referral links—at least 50% of all referral fees are pledged back into the $CRM and $cryptorugmunch project to build security / scam detection tools for the community.

• GMGN: https://gmgn.ai/r/rugmunch
• AXIOM: https://axiom.trade/@crmunch
• Trojan: https://t.me/solana_trojanbot?start=r-cryptorugmunch
• Photon: https://photon-sol.tinyastro.io/@cryptorugmunch
• Bloom: https://t.me/BloomSolana_bot?start=ref_YL9NHJBS9A
• BullX: https://t.me/BullxBetaBot?start=access_VD9LU4H5I2D
• Telemetry (by BONKbot): https://app.telemetry.io/@2dx3m

⚠️ Caution on Naked Crab Man - $CRABFURIE

• GMGN shows 1,000+ “fresh” wallets active, many funded in the last few days — classic bundle footprint.
• Devsnightmare bot: early buyers still hold ~14% of supply.
• TrenchRadar flags dozens of clustered wallets with clear associations; many are recently created.
• Seeing involvement from known pump‑and‑dump KOL scammers.

Conclusion: high insider concentration and coordinated wallet activity = elevated rug/scam risk. They may push it, but the structure looks predatory. Manage risk, size small, or avoid. Stay safe.

23:02
Crypto Rug Muncher
Big Announcement: Our project $CRM has officially been listed on CoinGecko! Congrats to the community!

While this listing is great news, there’s still some cleanup to do. I’ve submitted a request for @coingecko to update our market cap and remove an inaccurate @Rugcheckxyz warning on our project.

That warning appeared because our token metadata is marked as “mutable.” All this means is:

• The on-chain metadata (name/symbol/icon/URI) can be updated for branding fixes or logo changes.
• It does NOT allow minting more tokens, changing supply, or freezing user funds.
• Mint Authority and Freeze Authority are separate controls and are shown on Solscan.

Explorer (authorities):

https://solscan.io/token/Eme5T2s2HB7B8W4YgLG1eReQpnadEVUnQBRjaKTdBAGS#cards

We’ve provided all required info to CoinGecko, including supply and authorities, to ensure our market cap displays correctly and the RugCheck warning is removed. We’ll update you as soon as the changes go live. Thanks for the support, CRM fam!

Check out $CRM on CoinGecko:

https://coingecko.com/en/coins/crypto-rug-muncher

https://x.com/CryptoRugMunch/status/1971606157660651986

$CRM Chat
Folks thanks to anyone who attended the Space. Will be more focused in on my speaking speed moving forward. I am a English teacher by trade and understand some of you might not follow well if I speak too fast. Very passionate about our project but also need to slow down a bit when speaking so people can better understand me. Will work on improving this moving forward for the community. I've been a fast speaker my entire life and it's something that even bothers my own family sometimes lol.
❤

04:47
Crypto Rug Muncher
End of Week 1 Referral-Revenue Update

As promised, I’m committing at least 50% of referral revenue back into $CRM / $cryptorugmunch. Quick numbers from this first week on GMGN:

• Solana trading fees: $255
• BNB trading fees: $110
• Total: $365

To build good faith on this initiative, I’m donating 100% of these Week 1 earnings back into the project—every dollar. Consider this a down payment on what’s ahead.

We’re just getting started with referring trading platforms beyond GMGN, so there aren’t meaningful earnings to report yet. This is just a taste of what’s to come.

Sign up using our referral links below and route your trading through us to help build next‑gen security and scam‑detection tools for the community. Every trade through these links helps fund the growth of our project.

11:20
$CRM Chat
In reply to this message
The plan with the tokens would be to use them for eventual exchange listings, staking rewards etc. The CRM the Dao has can be verified at any time by checking rugmunchdao.sol. These tokens were purchased using the projects funds and I'll continually be buying more as the project moves forward. I also have CRM which I've bought with funds I've personally earned from this project in cryptorugmunch.sol. I'll not be selling any of my personal tokens either and if what I own gets to be excessive I'll contribute it to the Dao. Combined total holdings would probably put both wallets in the top 30 holders. I would like to increase this both personally and for the Dao so we have more control over the project later.

In terms of the Dao wallet I'm fine with multisigging it under the control of major stakeholders in the project once it grows. Right now it's got a grand or so in tokens in it so it's not a major concern but should it grow we'll not just be going the trust me bro route with the funds.
❤

23:33
Crypto Rug Muncher
I teased a major announcement—now it’s your call. This is a community-run project, so it isn’t my decision to make. There’s a migration platform that could theoretically preserve ~75–85% of liquidity (depending on community participation) and move $CRM to a contract we fully control (or to the Bonk ecosystem). It would include a 2-week window for current holders to swap old tokens for new. During that period, the original contract will continue trading as normal—you can trade in or out until you complete your swap. Should we migrate off @BagsApp? Leave your feedback and voice your concerns.

I’ll cover pros/cons in the AMA at 5 PM CST. I won’t move forward without broad community support. Personally, I think migration may be necessary eventually, but I won’t pursue it if it risks fracturing the community.
